mapping.a_element: a generic isolated quantum many-body system whose behavior is captured by a standard model
mapping.b_element: a Bose-Einstein condensate coupled to a cavity captured by the generic Dicke model
connection to the phase-transition boundary predicted by the model

brief_justification: The overlap is only generic vocabulary of quantum many-body physics captured by 'generic models'; the actual machinery differs. Paper A's mechanism is eigenstate thermalization (ETH) — relaxation of an isolated system to equilibrium encoded in individual eigenstates — whereas Paper B's is a Dicke quantum phase transition driven by infinitely long-ranged photon-mediated interactions with spontaneous symmetry breaking into a self-organized supersolid. Thermalization/relaxation dynamics and a symmetry-breaking phase transition are distinct governing processes with no role-for-role correspondence (no shared threshold, order parameter, or driving equation).
